meta_title: For Freelancers: content creation to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Content Creation to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn to leverage content creation and AI workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code to debug faster and ship client projects.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just about syntax; it's about context. By documenting your architectural decisions through structured content creation, you turn opaque errors into transparent logic flows. Use Antigravity to map dependencies and Claude Code to execute diagnostic reasoning, effectively eliminating the blind spots that stall your development speed.
content.intro:
The screen flickers, the terminal glows with a wall of red stack traces, and your client’s deadline is breathing down your neck. You are stuck in the 'debugging loop'—a productivity killer that turns high-value software engineering into a grueling game of trial and error. You aren't just losing time; you are losing your reputation as a fast, reliable builder.

But the secret to breaking this cycle isn't more coffee; it's externalizing your logic. By treating your debugging process as a form of content creation—where you document the 'why' alongside the 'how'—you force your brain to clarify intent. When you combine this discipline with the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform from a frantic debugger into a surgical architect who ships clean, robust code every single time.

steps.title: Map the Architectural Intent
steps.description: Before fixing the code, document the expected data flow. This creates a source of truth that AI can reference to identify deviations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current file structure and generate a mermaid flow diagram of the intended data lifecycle for this module.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --explain 'Map the expected data flow for the current module'

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Points
steps.description: Use content-driven logging to isolate the exact line of failure. Documenting the state of variables before and after execution provides the AI with context.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a diagnostic wrapper for this function that logs input/output states and identifies where the schema validation fails.

steps.title: Synthetic Reproduction
steps.description: Create a minimal reproduction script. If you can't describe the bug in a few sentences, you don't understand the bug yet.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a unit test suite that isolates this specific error scenario based on the logic documented in step 1.

steps.title: Automated Remediation
steps.description: Leverage Claude Code to propose a fix based on the documentation generated in previous steps. This ensures the fix aligns with the architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the proposed fix against the initial architectural diagram and suggest any necessary refactors for stability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --fix 'apply suggested patch and verify against test suite'

steps.title: Final Documentation Sync
steps.description: Update your technical documentation as content creation. This prevents future regressions and proves to the client you are a professional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Update the README and technical documentation to reflect the recent fix and the new error-handling logic implemented.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --commit 'chore: document bug fix and architectural update'

pro_tips: Always document the 'Negative Path'—what shouldn't happen is more important than what should.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to store common error patterns across different client projects.
Treat every bug report as a blog post draft; it forces you to explain the solution clearly.
Never ship a fix without a corresponding unit test; if it isn't tested, it's not a fix.
Keep your documentation directory inside your repo to ensure context is always available to your LLM.
content.next_steps: Begin by auditing your current project's documentation. If you find gaps, use the Antigravity mapping prompt to visualize your existing logic before touching the code.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the terminal and start building with precision. Our community of high-performing freelancers is waiting to share their workflows and help you scale your output. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
